nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
To send all the items back to the AI, we need to finish with everything aggregated into one single item.

Otherwise it will respond with one item at a time, and the AI will only get the first item that arrives.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
This node sends a custom HTTP Request to the Google Sheets API.

Filtering by date range in the Google Sheets API is very complicated.

This node solves that problem.

But doing the same in a database is much simpler. A tool could do it without needing a sub-workflow.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
The output from this complex request is also messy.

So we use some code generated by ChatGPT to transform the data into JSON objects.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Google Sheets request

parameters.name: tq
parameters.value: =SELECT * WHERE A >= DATE "{{ $json.start_date }}" AND A <= DATE "{{ $json.end_date }}"

parameters.content:
## Some questions to try
There's a red button on this page that you can click to chat with the AI.

Try asking it these questions:

- How many refunds in January and what was the amount refunded?

- How many successful sales did we have in January 2025 and what was the final income of those?

- What is the most frequent reason for refunds?

nodes.name: Sticky Note11

parameters.content:
## 👈 This is a special tool
It is used to call another workflow.
This concept is called sub-workflow.

See documentation [here]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Instead of running a completely separate workflow, we are calling the one below.

It's contained in the same workflow, but we are using the trigger to define it will run only when called by this tool.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note12

parameters.content:
# Sub-workflow
The AI can call this sub-workflow anytime,
by using the **Records by date** tool.

The sub-workflow automatically return
 the result of the last executed node to the AI.

parameters.content:
To send all the items back to the AI, we need to finish with everything aggregated into one single item.

Otherwise it will respond with one item at a time, and the AI will only get the first item that arrives.

This node sends a custom HTTP Request to the Google Sheets API.

Filtering by date range in the Google Sheets API is very complicated.

This node solves that problem.

But doing the same in a database is much simpler. A tool could do it without needing a sub-workflow.

# Author
![Solomon]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
### Solomon
Freelance consultant from Brazil, specializing in automations and data analysis. I work with select clients, addressing their toughest projects.

Currently running the [Scrapes community]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} with Simon 💪

For business inquiries, email me at automations.solomon@gmail.com
Or message me on [Telegram]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for a faster response.

## Check out my other templates
### 👉 {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## 👈 This is a special tool
It is used to call another workflow.
This concept is called sub-workflow.

See documentation [here]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Instead of running a completely separate workflow, we are calling the one below.

It's contained in the same workflow, but we are using the trigger to define it will run only when called by this tool.

# Sub-workflow
The AI can call this sub-workflow anytime,
by using the **Records by date** tool.

The sub-workflow automatically return
 the result of the last executed node to the AI.
## Change the URL of the Sheets file in all the Sheets tools 👇
## 👆 Change the URL of the Sheets file

The output from this complex request is also messy.

So we use some code generated by ChatGPT to transform the data into JSON objects.

## Some questions to try
There's a red button on this page that you can click to chat with the AI.

Try asking it these questions:

- How many refunds in January and what was the amount refunded?

- How many successful sales did we have in January 2025 and what was the final income of those?

- What is the most frequent reason for refunds?

## Copy this Sheets file to your Google Drive
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

You can use many models here, including the free Google Gemini options.

Make sure to test it thoroughly. Some models are better for data analysis.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When Executed by Another Workflow".
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Calculator" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Records by date" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Aggregate" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets request" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get transactions by product name" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Get all transactions" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note9" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note11" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Filter by status" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get transactions by status" Google Sheets node.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
